response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang backgrounds, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese (candidate lists English, Romanian, German), strong C for UNIX with Linux kernel source-modification experience (candidate had one brief C/C++ stint in 2014-2015), and deep L2/L3 networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, switch firmware) that is entirely absent from the resume. The hard language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Solid engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby
Linux proficiency and experience with performance-sensitive asynchronous I/O code
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement (native level)
No Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or network-switch/security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
C experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2014-2015); recent work is web/full-stack, far from embedded C for UNIX

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering seniority and Linux background
